message.content:
Review the following recently changed files in the CortexBuild Ultimate project at /var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate. Focus on bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, and adherence to project conventions. Use `git diff HEAD~5` to see the actual diff, then read the full files for context where needed.

Files to review:
- src/components/auth/LoginPage.tsx (full redesign - most important)
- server/routes/auth.js (new avatar, settings endpoints)
- server/routes/generic.js (ALLOWED_COLUMNS additions)
- server/lib/websocket.js (userId bug fix)
- src/hooks/useData.ts (query key corrections)
- src/components/modules/Settings.tsx (settings persistence)

Project context:
- React 18 + TypeScript strict + Vite frontend
- Express.js + PostgreSQL + JWT backend
- TanStack React Query v5 for data fetching
- All components use dark theme (bg-slate-900, bg-slate-800 etc.)
- No CLAUDE.md exists, use general best practices

Return findings categorized as: Critical Issues, Important Issues, Suggestions.

content.text: I'll start by examining the git diff and project structure, then read the full files for context.

input.description: Show diff stats for last 5 commits

90ea6e8 feat: redesign login page with split-screen blueprint hero layout
ff3d643 fix: comprehensive bug fixes, DB migration, and settings persistence
78b1535 chore: add build artifacts to .gitignore
9c4b5dd fix: remove all remaining light-theme classes from 6 modules
2ddb398 fix: upgrade bcrypt to v6 to resolve 3 high-severity tar CVEs
30f89d6 feat: implement self-registration, fix login, full dark theme, live data wiring
d8f4848 docs: add README with setup instructions
2318f00 chore: update .env.example for Express backend
8733d09 chore: remove obsolete files and build artifacts
c89b3ec feat: full platform redesign — dark theme + Express backend migration

Here is my review. --- ## Code Review: CortexBuild Ultimate (HEAD~5) ### Files Reviewed - `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/src/components/auth/LoginPage.tsx` - `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/routes/auth.js` - `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/routes/generic.js` - `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/lib/websocket.js` - `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/src/hooks/useData.ts` - `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/src/components/modules/Settings.tsx` - `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/index.js` - `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/middleware/auth.js` --- ### Critical Issues (90-100) **1. Mismatched JWT secret fallback values -- WebSocket authentication will silently fail in development** [Confidence: 95] File: `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/lib/websocket.js`, line 9 File: `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/routes/auth.js`, line 8 File: `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/middleware/auth.js`, line 10 Three different fallback values are used when `process.env.JWT_SECRET` is not set: - `websocket.js`: `'cortexbuild-secret-key'` - `auth.js`: `'cortexbuild_secret'` - `middleware/auth.js`: `'cortexbuild_secret'` If `JWT_SECRET` is not in the environment (which can happen in local development or if the `.env` file is misconfigured), `auth.js` will sign tokens with `'cortexbuild_secret'` but `websocket.js` will verify them with `'cortexbuild-secret-key'`. Every WebSocket connection will be rejected with a 4001 Unauthorized close code, with no obvious error indicating the cause since `verifyToken` only logs "Token verification failed" without details. Fix: Use a single shared constant or module for the JWT secret, or at minimum ensure all fallback values are identical. --- **2. Self-delete guard uses strict equality between string and integer types** [Confidence: 92] File: `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/routes/auth.js`, line 176 ```js if (req.params.id === req.user.id) return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Cannot delete your own account' }); ``` `req.params.id` is always a string (from the URL path), while `req.user.id` comes from the JWT payload and is a number (PostgreSQL integer `id`). The strict equality `===` will never be true (e.g., `"5" === 5` is `false`), so this safety guard is completely bypassed -- users can delete their own accounts. Fix: Use `String(req.user.id)` for comparison, or convert `req.params.id` with `parseInt()`, or use loose equality `==`. --- **3. PUT /api/auth/avatar accepts arbitrary URL with no validation -- Stored XSS / SSRF vector** [Confidence: 90] File: `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/routes/auth.js`, lines 188-200 The avatar endpoint accepts any string as the avatar value and stores it directly in the database. There is no URL format validation, no allowlist of domains, and no sanitization. Since this value is presumably rendered in `` on the frontend, a `javascript:` URI or data URI containing script could be injected. Additionally, if the server ever fetches the avatar URL server-side, this is an SSRF vector. Fix: Validate that the avatar value is a well-formed URL starting with `https://`, ideally against an allowlist of accepted domains or your own upload endpoint. --- **4. PUT /api/auth/settings accepts arbitrary keys -- unbounded data injection** [Confidence: 90] File: `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/routes/auth.js`, lines 217-231 The settings endpoint accepts any `key` string and stores it in the `app_settings` table with no validation or allowlist. A malicious authenticated user can insert unlimited arbitrary keys, potentially causing storage exhaustion or using the settings store as an arbitrary data dump. There is also no length limit on the `value` (which is cast to `jsonb`). Fix: Validate `key` against an allowlist of known setting keys (e.g., `['notifications', 'theme', 'company']`) and impose a reasonable size limit on `value`. --- ### Important Issues (80-89) **5. WebSocket broadcast event has no authorization -- any authenticated user can broadcast to all connected clients** [Confidence: 85] File: `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/server/lib/websocket.js`, lines 234-244 The `broadcast` event handler in `handleMessage` allows any authenticated WebSocket client to send a message to every connected user. There is no role check or rate limiting. An attacker with valid credentials (even a `field_worker` role) could flood all users with arbitrary messages. Fix: Add role-based authorization (e.g., restrict broadcast to `super_admin` or `company_owner`) or remove the broadcast event from client-initiated messages entirely. --- **6. LoginPage uses window.innerWidth for responsive layout -- not reactive, SSR-unsafe** [Confidence: 82] File: `/var/www/cortexbuild-ultimate/src/components/auth/LoginPage.tsx`, line 317 ```tsx ...(typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.innerWidth > 900 ? { display: 'flex' } : {}), ``` This reads `window.innerWidth` once at render time and never updates when the window is resized. The hero panel will not appear or disappear on resize. However, the component also includes a `
